title: Regulatory Drift
description: SR 26-2, the supersession of SR 11-7, and what effective challenge actually requires when the model under review is a GenAI system.

title: Agentic Engineering
description: How harness design, guardrails, and skills supply-chain security determine whether agentic systems are actually governable.

title: Agent to Agent
description: A2A protocol design, cross-agent security risks, and real-world case studies of agentic systems in the wild.

title: GUI Agents
description: MolmoWeb's training data inversion result, open-source harness gaps, and the Universal Verifier's backbone-swap finding.

title: The Eval Gap
description: Measurement theory, NIST benchmark frameworks, psychometrics, and why most LLM eval pipelines report the wrong uncertainty estimate.

title: Adversarial Testing
description: Five-phase red-teaming, three deployment gates, and why adversarial completeness is provably unachievable — and why you still need the program.

title: Human and the Loop
description: Guardrail design, sixty years of human oversight vocabulary, and how HITL timing regimes map to MRM governance obligations.

title: Bainbridge's Debt
description: Forty years of HCI and human-factors research has named, empirically documented, and partially solved banking's human-automation failure modes. Banking keeps ignoring it.

title: Banking AI
description: Rail-by-rail liability gaps, agent identity under CIP rules, and the regulatory vacuum agentic commerce is already operating inside.

title: Economics of AI
description: Comparative advantage models, token transfer pricing, and knowledge collapse — the macro and institutional economics of AI adoption.
